42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.

1974	/-- A ring hom `φ : A → K` into the fraction field `K` of a domain `R`, with image contained in the
1975	subring `(algebraMap R K).range`, corestricts to a ring hom `A → R`. Project-local helper for
1976	assembling `g'` (the valuation-ring factorisation of the transported `K`-point) in step E5. -/
1977	private noncomputable def liftToBaseOfMemRange {A R K : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing R]
1978	    [Field K] [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K] (φ : A →+* K)
1979	    (hmem : ∀ x, φ x ∈ (algebraMap R K).range) : A →+* R :=
1980	  letI hinj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict := fun a b h =>
1981	    IsFractionRing.injective R K (by
1982	      have hv := congrArg Subtype.val h
1983	      rwa [RingHom.coe_rangeRestrict, RingHom.coe_rangeRestrict] at hv)
1984	  (RingEquiv.ofBijective (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict
1985	    ⟨hinj, (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict_surjective⟩).symm.toRingHom.comp
1986	    (φ.codRestrict (algebraMap R K).range hmem)

1989	structure map `R ↪ K` recovers the original `φ`. Project-local. -/
1990	private lemma algebraMap_comp_liftToBaseOfMemRange {A R K : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing R]
1991	    [Field K] [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K] (φ : A →+* K)
1992	    (hmem : ∀ x, φ x ∈ (algebraMap R K).range) :
1993	    (algebraMap R K).comp (liftToBaseOfMemRange φ hmem) = φ := by
1994	  letI hinj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict := fun a b h =>
1995	    IsFractionRing.injective R K (by
1996	      have hv := congrArg Subtype.val h
1997	      rwa [RingHom.coe_rangeRestrict, RingHom.coe_rangeRestrict] at hv)
1998	  set e := RingEquiv.ofBijective (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict
1999	    ⟨hinj, (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict_surjective⟩ with he
2000	  ext x
2001	  change algebraMap R K (e.symm (φ.codRestrict (algebraMap R K).range hmem x)) = φ x
2002	  have happ : (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict (e.symm (φ.codRestrict (algebraMap R K).range hmem x))
2003	      = φ.codRestrict (algebraMap R K).range hmem x := by
2004	    rw [← RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict
2005	      ⟨hinj, (algebraMap R K).rangeRestrict_surjective⟩, ← he, RingEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
2006	  have hv := congrArg Subtype.val happ
2007	  rwa [RingHom.coe_rangeRestrict] at hv

2009	/-- **E5 — the existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ`**
2010	(`lem:gr_valuativeExistence_toSpecZ`): every valuative square over the structure morphism
2011	`toSpecZ : Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` admits a diagonal lift. Assembles the chart factorization (E1,
2012	`existence_chart_factorization`), the minimal-valuation chart selection (E2,
2013	`existence_minimal_valuation`), the valuation-ring factorization of the transported `K`-point (E3,
2014	`existence_factor_through_valuationRing`, corestricted to `g'` via `liftToBaseOfMemRange`), and the
2015	filler (E4, `existence_lift`). Project-local: the genuine geometric content of properness; feeds
2016	`isProper_of_valuativeExistence` to close `Grassmannian.isProper`. -/
2017	theorem valuativeExistence_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) :
2018	    ValuativeCriterion.Existence (toSpecZ d r) := by
2019	  intro S
2020	  -- E1: factor the generic-point morphism through a chart `I`.
2021	  obtain ⟨I, f, hi₁⟩ := existence_chart_factorization d r S.i₁
2022	  -- E2: select the minimal-valuation chart `J` (`f (P^I_J) ≠ 0`).
2023	  obtain ⟨J, hJmax, hne⟩ := existence_minimal_valuation d r (R := S.R) I f
2024	  -- In the field `K`, nonzero means unit.
2025	  have hf : IsUnit (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr hne
2026	  -- E3: the transported `K`-point `g` lands in `R`; corestrict to `g'`.
2027	  have hmem := existence_factor_through_valuationRing d r I J f hJmax hf
2028	  have hg' : (algebraMap S.R S.K).comp
2029	        (liftToBaseOfMemRange
2030	          ((IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf).comp
2031	            (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom) hmem)
2032	      = (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf).comp
2033	          (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom :=
2034	    algebraMap_comp_liftToBaseOfMemRange _ hmem
2035	  -- E4: the filler is a lift structure for the square.
2036	  exact ⟨⟨existence_lift d r I J S.i₁ S.i₂ S.commSq f hi₁ hf _ hg'⟩⟩
2037	
2038	/-- **The Grassmannian `Gr(d,r)` is proper over `ℤ`** (`lem:gr_proper`): the structure morphism
2039	`toSpecZ : Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is proper. The three "cheap" valuative-criterion ingredients and the
2040	uniqueness half are discharged by `isProper_of_valuativeExistence`; the existence half is
2041	`valuativeExistence_toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1, "Properness"). Project-local. -/
2042	theorem isProper (d r : ℕ) : IsProper (toSpecZ d r) :=
2043	  isProper_of_valuativeExistence d r (valuativeExistence_toSpecZ d r)
2044

129

131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is

9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).

59

61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
